report_title: Daily Market Briefing — Mandala Sustainable Solutions
executive_summary: Market Condition: Neutral — Equities rallied on stable macro; breadth improving as rotation continues from mega-cap tech into semiconductors and industrials
Key Signal: SPY +0.78% and QQQ +1.55% after softer-than-expected June jobs data reinforces Fed cut expectations; broader participation improving
Risk to Watch: Sector concentration risk remains despite rally; SaaS structural concerns persist; geopolitical oil premium intact
Cross-Asset: Bonds stable as yield curve steepens further; gold steady $4,150; dollar weakening on rate cut expectations (-0.68%)

sectors.strength: Semiconductors (AI infrastructure resilience despite 4.5% intra-sector weakness), Industrials, Energy (oil supply concerns)
sectors.weakness: Software/SaaS (structural AI disruption), Real Estate (rate sensitive), Utilities (end demand softening)

equities.synthesis: Equities found support on stable macro data; SPY +0.78% and QQQ +1.55% show broadening participation beyond mega-cap darlings. Sector rotation accelerating as investors rotate out of rate-sensitive tech into cyclicals and hard assets. Small caps lagging (IWM -0.07%) signals risk-off bias for lower-quality names.

treasury_10y.context: Stable; market pricing Fed cuts for H2

fixed_income.synthesis: Curve steepening continues as market prices Fed easing cycle. TLT rallying (+0.78%) on falling long-end yields signals investors front-running rate cut narrative. HY spreads compressed at 275 bps — watch for widening if growth concerns resurface; credit market not pricing recession risk adequately.

copper.signal: Holding key support; bullish while above $5.25/lb

commodities.synthesis: Oil +0.83% to $69.18 on Strait of Hormuz geopolitical concerns; upside risks to energy prices remain. Dollar weakening sharply (-0.68%) on Fed rate cut expectations—classic risk-on signal. Gold retreat (-0.41%) reflects easing geopolitical bid; copper holding structural support level, showing resilience on tech/AI infrastructure demand.

macro.fed_stance: Hold likely; cuts priced for late July/August

CPI.context: Above target; June data due July 14

macro.synthesis: June jobs data softening (215K claims) is the catalyst shifting Fed expectations toward H2 cuts. ISM Manufacturing at 53.3 continues moderation story—expansion intact but losing momentum. Inflation remains sticky at 4.2% YoY (May), but rate cut market is discounting a easing cycle starting late July. FOMC minutes this week will be critical for signaling timing and magnitude of cuts.

watchpoints: FOMC Minutes released this week — any hawkish surprise would reverse Fed cut narrative and pressure equities sharply
June CPI due Tuesday July 14 at 8:30 AM ET — market expecting moderation; data above expectations could derail rate cut bets
Sector breadth — monitor if small caps (IWM) stabilize or continue lagging; indicates risk appetite sustainability
Oil geopolitical escalation — watch if Strait of Hormuz tensions worsen; oil could spike above $72 quickly
Tech earnings guidance — Q2 results rolling in; watch for SaaS margin pressure and AI displacement commentary
